title: Unable to install Podman on Ubutu 20.04
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
Unable to install podman on Ubutu 20.04

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Follow steps on podman site - https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
2. This was working this week and for the past 1 year, just started to break last day or so.  


**Describe the results you received:**
curl -fsSL https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/xUbuntu_$(lsb_release -rs)/Release.key \
>   | gpg --dearmor \
>   | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/devel_kubic_libcontainers_unstable.gpg > /dev/null
curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 404 

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman gets installed


```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes

comments.body: I believe this is also the issue for another Ubuntu releases (I've faced that in i.e 18.04). It would be good to fix this relatively quickly due to fact that this problem may affect a lot of environments.
Could it be that `unstable` isn't supported by Ubuntu 20.04 and 18.04?

Please try this link:
https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/unstable/xUbuntu_22.04/Release.key 
It looks like the URLs have changed.

To me it seems that `unstable` corresponds to Podman 4 while `stable` is Podman 3.

https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/amd64/
https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/unstable/xUbuntu_22.04/amd64/

If Podman 4 would not be supported by Ubuntu 20.04 for some reason (e.g. Kernel version requirements) you could not use the `unstable` branch with Ubuntu 20.04 and you probably had to change `unstable` to `stable` in your URL.

Yes I have tried switching the url to unstable.  The issue is the certificate that comes back has expired


We started with the stable that then redirects to the unstable and then that goes to a URL That's a 404.


Playing with all the features we get to a state where I don't get a 404 within the cert that comes back is expired. Looks like it expired on December 5th.
